hero.title: I experiment with technology, blend it with design, and innovate for human needs.

aiChat.greeting: Hello! I'm a digital consciousness, trained on this portfolio. Ask me anything about the creator's work or thoughts.
aiChat.promptPlaceholder: Ask about my work, skills, philosophy...

about.title: ABOUT ME
about.paragraph1: As a designer, I am driven by the convergence of disparate fields. I thrive on combining, blending, and innovating with emerging technologies to create experiences that are not just useful, but meaningful. My process is a dialogue between human needs and technological possibilities.
about.paragraph2: Click on a cell in the grid to generate a unique design philosophy statement, powered by a simple AI model trained on my work and ethos.

philosophies.statement: Design is a medium for conversation, not just a solution.
Technology should be an extension of human capability, not a replacement.
Embrace ambiguity and complexity to find novel insights.
The most powerful experiences are born from interdisciplinary collaboration.
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication in digital experiences.
Every pixel should serve a purpose beyond aesthetics.
Innovation happens at the intersection of constraints and creativity.
Human-centered design begins with empathy and ends with impact.
The best interfaces disappear, leaving only the experience.
Accessibility is not a feature, it's a fundamental right.
Sustainable design considers both user needs and planetary impact.
Failure is the best teacher in the design process.
Context is king in user experience design.
The future of design is collaborative and inclusive.

bio.intro: I'm a passionate product designer and technologist who believes in the power of interdisciplinary collaboration to solve complex human problems. With a background spanning design, technology, and innovation, I create experiences that bridge the gap between human needs and technological possibilities.
bio.mission: My mission is to design products and experiences that not only solve problems but also inspire and empower people to achieve their full potential. I believe in creating technology that serves humanity, not the other way around.
bio.approach: I approach design as a conversation between constraints and creativity, between user needs and technological capabilities. Every project is an opportunity to learn, iterate, and push the boundaries of what's possible.

education.description: Specialized in Product Design Development and Management

experience.description: Leading design initiatives for aerospace technology interfaces, focusing on safety and user experience in high-stakes environments.
Designed user experiences for web applications and mobile apps, collaborating with development teams to implement design solutions.
Worked on game design and interactive experiences, learning the fundamentals of user-centered design and rapid prototyping.
Manufacturing and Repair of BOXNHL Wagons

aiPhilosophy.context: As a designer, I am driven by the convergence of disparate fields. I thrive on combining, blending, and innovating with emerging technologies to create experiences that are not just useful, but meaningful. My process is a dialogue between human needs and technological possibilities. I believe design is a medium for conversation, technology should be an extension of human capability, ambiguity should be embraced, and collaboration is key.
aiPhilosophy.promptTemplate: Generate a concise design philosophy statement (under 15 words) based on the following context: {context}. The statement should feel insightful and unique.

projects.title: Airbus
projects.description: I was responsible for designing and developing the gamified system and user interface, working closely with engineering, business, and operations teams to understand their needs and create an engaging user experience for a wide range of users.
projects.designProcess: The project aims to create a gamified learning environment for new joiners in the Flight Physics department. The platform will use adaptive learning pathways to personalize training, ensuring each member upskills effectively while avoiding redundancy. Through interactive challenges, rewards, and real-world problem scenarios, the system will accelerate onboarding, build knowledge of cross-functional dependencies, and align learners with industry standards and Airbus’ business pipeline.
projects.technicalDetails: The objective of the project was to upscale processes to meet industry standards, while gaining exposure to cross-functional dependencies and developing a comprehensive understanding of the Airbus business pipeline.

sections.content: I was responsible for designing and developing the gamified system and user interface, working closely with engineering, business, and operations teams to understand their needs and create an engaging user experience for a wide range of users. My primary responsibility was the system-level design, building the complete ecosystem where departments such as aerodynamics, capabilities, operations, and business seamlessly integrate into a unified gamified environment—a crucial requirement of the project. To achieve this, I collaborated extensively with core Flight Physics engineers and programmers to ensure the system was both functional and impactful.
This project is still ongoing and comes under Airbus NDA policies, so I can’t share full details here. But I’d be happy to discuss it in more detail in a closed conversation. Let’s connect!

projects.title: Recall
projects.description: A tool for collaborative world-building for writers and game designers.
projects.fullDescription: Recall is an AI-powered app designed to help you capture, track, and cherish your memories in a unique and visually engaging way. It seamlessly blends AI note-taking with the creation of stunning visuals, allowing you to build a rich and personal timeline of your life's most precious moments.
projects.designProcess: The design process for Recall began with a simple, yet profound, observation: memories are often fragmented, vivid in parts, and sometimes fade over time. We remember snippets, feelings, and key moments, but the full tapestry can be elusive. The initial spark was born from the desire to not just record information, but to genuinely re-experience life's moments.
projects.technicalDetails: This application is a modern, serverless web app built with a fast Vite and TypeScript frontend and styled with Tailwind CSS. For the backend, it cleverly avoids traditional databases. User identity is managed securely through Google Authentication. All personal memories and notes are stored directly in the user's own Google Drive, making data storage free, private, and entirely under the user's control. This approach also provides automatic, seamless synchronization of all memories across any device where the user is logged into their Google account.

projects.title: CodeFryDev
projects.description: CodeFryDev is your ultimate destination for diverse digital content and tools. Enjoy our extensive game collection, innovative productivity apps, ...
projects.designProcess: We partnered with local historians to gather archival content. The UX challenge was to present this information in an engaging way without overwhelming the user. We used narrative threads to guide users on historical walks.
projects.technicalDetails: The app uses ARKit for iOS and ARCore for Android. 3D models of historical scenes were created in Blender and optimized for mobile performance.

sections.content: CodeFryDev specializing in producing a wide array of digital products. The company focuses on creating games, apps, and various tools, while also providing educational materials and tutorials. Their work spans projects in C# development, deep learning, and computer vision, and they offer a range of creative assets for other developers to utilize

projects.title: Phygital Projects
projects.description: the strategic integration of the physical and digital worlds to create more engaging, immersive, and personalized customer experiences.
projects.fullDescription: This page contains different phygital projects that i have worked on which includes the seamless integration of both physical and digital experiences to create a more immersive and user-friendly interaction.

customBlocks.content: This project is an interactive simulation of a robotic arm controlled in 3D space by a custom-built, motion-sensing air mouse. The user physically moves the air mouse, and the on-screen robotic arm mimics the motion to perform tasks in a training game. The system is designed to be highly modular, allowing for easy customization of the arm's structure, controls, and game mechanics.

Prism (Present Resonance Interface for Sensory Mindfulness) is a stylish, minimalist wearable device designed to gently nudge users back to the present moment through subtle sensory cues. It combines haptic feedback, soft light, and a companion app to cultivate mindfulness without screen dependency.

This project was a six-week sprint from idea to a fully functional mindfulness product, designed to help users stay present in the moment by addressing chronic digital distraction and cognitive overload.
From initial research and ideation to final product execution and promotional planning, every stage was optimized for efficiency, proving that impactful design doesn’t require a high budget—just the right vision and execution.

Throughout the development process, I tackled multiple challenges, including rapid prototyping, user testing, marketing, and financial constraints. With a strict budget of ₹400,I focused on cost-effective materials, minimalistic yet engaging design, and creative marketing strategies to ensure accessibility and scalability.

ReactoSphere is a compact, user-friendly device designed to measure human reflex responses with ±1ms accuracy. Built around an ATmega328P microcontroller, it features a 4-digit 7-segment display for real-time feedback, green and red LEDs for visual cues, and tactile buttons for input. Upon startup, a rotating dash animation transitions into a 3-2-1 countdown, followed by the display of the best score stored in non-volatile EEPROM memory. During operation, a random 2–3s delay prevents anticipation before activating the red LED, with reaction time calculated from the LED activation to button press. The device handles errors (early presses/timeouts) gracefully, displaying Err1 or FULL, and incorporates a long-press reset (>2s) to reboot or clear scores. Designed for durability and safety, it uses current-limiting resistors (220Ω for LEDs, 330Ω for display segments), debounced button inputs, and low-power components, making it ideal for sports training, cognitive assessments, and competitive gaming.

projects.title: Generative Modeling
projects.description: This page contains projects that are related to generative modeling.
projects.fullDescription: This page contains some experimental project that i have worked on , It includes generative modeling, computational design, procedural modeling, and AI.

customBlocks.content: BikeGen is a generative design tool that allows you to design and create your own bicycles in no time, it is fully procedural, you can customize the design parameters according to your needs. This project is still in development and i am working on it to make it more effective and efficient. This project is based on Unity C# programming languagge.  Initially i started this project in Blender using python script , but later i switched to unity , because as i was adding more and more features Blender was kind of slwo or crashing sometimes. So i decided to switch to unity , as it is a game engine it can handle lots of calculations easily.

From initial research and ideation to final product execution and promotional planning, every stage was optimized for efficiency, proving that impactful design doesn’t require a high budget—just the right vision and execution.

experimentalProjects.title: Bengaluru Bat Craft
experimentalProjects.description: The Cricket Bat in Bengaluru: A Semiotic Study of Meaning, Emotion, and Socioeconomic Context Among Non-Professional Players
experimentalProjects.fullDescription: The Cricket Bat in Bengaluru: A Semiotic Study of Meaning, Emotion, and Socioeconomic Context Among Non-Professional Players
experimentalProjects.designProcess: This study uses a qualitative, multi-stage approach to uncover the meanings of the cricket bat among non-professional players in Bengaluru. It begins with literature review and contextual research, followed by field observations of amateur cricket across economic groups. Then, semi-structured interviews explore personal stories and emotional attachments, while artifact analysis examines the physical bats themselves. The data is then coded through thematic analysis, deepened using semiotic square analysis to map symbolic oppositions, and finally compared across groups in a cross-case analysis to reveal how socioeconomic context shapes the bat’s meanings.
experimentalProjects.technicalDetails: The research is grounded in semiotic theory (Saussurean and Peircean traditions, denotation/connotation, Greimas’ semiotic square) and material culture studies. It applies qualitative techniques such as thematic analysis (reflexive coding, inductive and deductive approaches), artifact analysis of cricket bats, and cross-case comparison. These methods enable decoding of symbolic meanings, emotional associations, and socioeconomic markers embedded in the cricket bat.

blogPosts.title: The Future of AI in Design
blogPosts.description: Exploring how artificial intelligence is reshaping the design landscape and what it means for designers.
blogPosts.fullDescription: Artificial intelligence is fundamentally changing how we approach design. From automated layout generation to intelligent user research analysis, AI tools are becoming an integral part of the modern designer's toolkit. But what does this mean for the future of design as a human-centered discipline?
blogPosts.designProcess: This article explores the intersection of AI and design through research, case studies, and personal experimentation. I've spent months testing various AI design tools and analyzing their impact on creative workflows and outcomes.
blogPosts.technicalDetails: The research covers tools like Midjourney, DALL-E, and various AI-powered design assistants. I've documented the technical capabilities, limitations, and practical applications of these technologies in real design projects.

sections.content: The key insight from this research is that AI should augment human creativity rather than replace it. The most successful implementations combine AI's computational power with human intuition and emotional intelligence.

blogPosts.title: Designing for the Metaverse
blogPosts.description: A deep dive into the unique challenges and opportunities of designing immersive 3D experiences.
blogPosts.fullDescription: As we move towards a more immersive digital future, designers are facing new challenges in creating experiences that exist in three-dimensional space. The metaverse represents a fundamental shift from 2D interfaces to spatial computing, requiring new design principles and methodologies.
blogPosts.designProcess: This exploration involved creating several 3D prototypes and testing them in VR environments. I focused on understanding how traditional design principles translate to spatial interfaces and what new considerations emerge in 3D design.
blogPosts.technicalDetails: The research utilized Unity and Unreal Engine for prototyping, along with various VR headsets for testing. I documented the technical challenges of 3D interface design and the solutions that emerged from iterative testing.

sections.content: Spatial design requires rethinking fundamental UX principles. Navigation, information hierarchy, and user interaction patterns all need to be adapted for three-dimensional space while maintaining intuitive usability.

blogPosts.title: Sustainable Design in the Digital Age
blogPosts.description: How digital designers can contribute to environmental sustainability through thoughtful design decisions.
blogPosts.fullDescription: Digital design might seem disconnected from environmental concerns, but our design choices have real environmental impacts. From energy-efficient code to sustainable hosting solutions, designers have numerous opportunities to reduce the carbon footprint of digital products.
blogPosts.designProcess: This research involved analyzing the environmental impact of various digital products and services. I examined energy consumption, server efficiency, and the lifecycle of digital assets to understand how design decisions affect sustainability.
blogPosts.technicalDetails: The study covered topics like green hosting, energy-efficient algorithms, and sustainable design practices. I documented specific techniques and tools that designers can use to create more environmentally friendly digital experiences.

sections.content: Every design decision matters. From choosing energy-efficient hosting to optimizing images and code, designers can significantly reduce the environmental impact of digital products while maintaining excellent user experiences.

blogPosts.title: The Psychology of Color in Digital Interfaces
blogPosts.description: Understanding how color choices affect user behavior and emotional responses in digital products.
blogPosts.fullDescription: Color is one of the most powerful tools in a designer's arsenal, capable of influencing user emotions, behavior, and perception. Understanding the psychological impact of color choices is crucial for creating effective digital interfaces that resonate with users on an emotional level.
blogPosts.designProcess: This research involved extensive user testing with different color schemes and analyzing behavioral data. I focused on understanding how color affects user engagement, conversion rates, and overall user satisfaction across different contexts and cultures.
blogPosts.technicalDetails: The study utilized eye-tracking technology, user surveys, and A/B testing to gather data on color preferences and their impact on user behavior. I documented specific color combinations and their psychological effects.

sections.content: The research revealed that color psychology is highly contextual and cultural. What works in one market or application may not work in another, making it essential for designers to understand their specific user base and cultural context.

blogPosts.title: Micro-interactions: The Secret to Delightful UX
blogPosts.description: How small, thoughtful interactions can transform user experience from functional to delightful.
blogPosts.fullDescription: Micro-interactions are the small, often overlooked details that make digital experiences feel polished and delightful. From button hover states to loading animations, these tiny interactions can significantly impact user satisfaction and engagement.
blogPosts.designProcess: This exploration involved creating and testing various micro-interaction patterns across different digital products. I focused on understanding the balance between functionality and delight, ensuring that animations enhance rather than hinder user experience.
blogPosts.technicalDetails: The research covered CSS animations, JavaScript libraries, and various prototyping tools. I documented specific micro-interaction patterns and their implementation details, along with performance considerations and accessibility requirements.

sections.content: The key to successful micro-interactions is subtlety and purpose. Every animation should serve a functional purpose while adding a layer of polish that makes users feel that the product was crafted with care and attention to detail.
